tagline: Player-first reference pages for the live Pazneria build: encounters, named NPC Talk-to dialogue, starter-town homesteads, the first quest, and the systems around them.
footerNote: Reference-first today, with room for deeper quest breakdowns, lore, and encounter walkthroughs once the export-backed core is fully polished.
intro: The codex is designed to answer practical player questions quickly: what an item does, how a skill loop works, what an encounter drops, which NPCs can actually be talked to, and what a region offers right now.
It stays grounded in the canonical game export bundle so stable IDs, dialogue hooks, appearance IDs, routes, services, encounter data, and cross-links remain aligned with the source project.

homeSections.title: Encounter Pages
homeSections.body: Enemy pages now read like live encounter references, with danger level, drops, roaming, and respawn details surfaced in the same codex voice as the rest of the site.

homeSections.title: Talk-to Dialogue
homeSections.body: Named NPC coverage now reflects the live Talk-to layer, so dialogue-backed services read as characters with interactions instead of silent utility markers.

homeSections.title: Starter-Town Homesteads
homeSections.body: Starter Town is no longer just a service checklist. The codex now needs to read it as a settlement with homes, yards, workshops, and profession plots around named NPCs.

homeSections.title: First Live Quest
homeSections.body: The home portal should make it obvious that the project has crossed into quest territory, with the first live quest standing alongside training loops and reference pages.

homeSections.title: Linked Systems
homeSections.body: Items, skills, worlds, enemies, dialogue hooks, and quest-facing pages cross-link through stable IDs so the codex stays dependable as the project grows.
